objective: Allow existing super_admin and content_admin to durably delete only conversion_failed resources through a public-id command, atomically detach metadata and enqueue a redacted cleanup job, safely retry exact local object deletion under the same object lock used by upload receipt and conversion, and preserve idempotent response-loss recovery without changing resource authorization, successful resource lifecycle, upload formats, or external storage.
blockedFiles: all schema migration product and test files outside the exact allowedFiles list
historical evidence audit task-plan and archive files
migration apply real database real data external storage Provider deployment PR force-push and non-canonical remote

preRedImpactScan.authoritativeChain: admin conversion_failed row action -> resource public-id DELETE route -> existing content-admin session boundary -> resource repository transaction and deterministic object advisory lock -> resource cleanup outbox -> strict local resource delete primitive -> retry outcome and redacted audit
preRedImpactScan.confirmedRootCause: conversion_failed resources retain a completed upload operation and private content-addressed file but have no authorized delete command, durable cleanup identity, shared object lock, or response-loss retry projection

cleanupContract.scope: Only conversion_failed resource rows may be deleted; uploaded converting draft published indexing index_failed rag_ready and disabled fail closed.
cleanupContract.authorization: Existing super_admin or content_admin authorization must complete before resource lookup, cleanup lookup, local file access, or audit detail access; no role or entitlement expansion.
cleanupContract.durability: Resource metadata detach, relation cleanup, completed upload-operation tombstone, redacted delete audit, and cleanup job enqueue occur atomically in one transaction.
cleanupContract.concurrency: Upload receipt, conversion claim/finalize, delete, and cleanup for an exact objectStoragePath share one deterministic PostgreSQL transaction advisory-lock namespace and consistent lock order.
cleanupContract.references: Any resource row or pending/file_stored upload operation with the exact object identity prevents deletion; inconsistent identity for the same object path fails closed.
cleanupContract.retry: The same public-id DELETE command resumes pending or failed cleanup, treats an exact missing target idempotently, never duplicates success audit, and converges after response loss or crash windows.
cleanupContract.localDelete: Only the full repository-provided dev/resource/{profession}/{YYYYMM}/{sha256}.{extension} identity may be deleted after canonical-root, realpath, symlink, type, size, and streaming SHA-256 verification; no recursive or broad deletion.
cleanupContract.residual: No background dispatcher, deployment-wide cleanup worker, migration apply, real database proof, or external object storage is claimed.

hardConditions: T1 commits conversion_failed metadata deletion upload tombstone redacted success audit and cleanup enqueue before any physical unlink
T2 independently claims the cleanup job acquires the exact object advisory lock rechecks references live uploads and identity then unlinks and finalizes while the lock remains held
prepare upload conversion claim and finalize delete and cleanup use one deterministic resource object lock namespace and lock order
cleanup is durable stale-claim recoverable idempotent and never revives a deleted resource for the same upload idempotency key
authorization precedes resource cleanup file and audit access and unexpected graph relations fail closed
strict local delete consumes only repository-owned identity and synthetic isolated files

migrationFormatPolicy: Drizzle-generated SQL is excluded from Prettier because no SQL parser is configured; the focused migration-source test validates exact additive structure and git diff --check validates whitespace.
closeoutConditions: guardian task-bound approval binds this exact base envelope risk and conditional closeout before RED
actual diff remains within the exact envelope and no migration or real database execution occurs
TDD and adversarial review prove authorization object lock reference checks durable retry strict deletion redaction and no successful-resource deletion
focused tests lint typecheck build format diff schema-source state and main-thread review pass
one reviewable product-partition commit ff-only canonical push sync and cleanup
F-0082 remains open_partitioned only for durable background dispatcher deployment-wide budgets and OS containment unless independent acceptance proves otherwise

validationEvidence.red: 1 file and 13 tests failed before implementation for the missing DELETE route cleanup schema shared lock and strict local delete
validationEvidence.build: passed with the pre-existing Next NFT trace warning
validationEvidence.schemaMigrationSource: additive enum table indexes and check only; snapshot parent and journal verified
validationEvidence.adversarialReview: passed with authorization-first, T1-before-unlink, shared object-lock, relation/live-upload fail-closed, tombstone replay, exact-once audit, strict identity and no envelope drift
